title: Senator Cortese issued the following statement on the passage of SB 553 from the Assembly Appropriations Committee, a bill to protect workers from workplace violence:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill (SB) 553 requires employers to develop their own workplace violence prevention plans as part of their Cal/OSHA Injury Illness Prevention Plan.",
    "Employers must maintain a Violent Incident Log of all violent incidents against employees and conduct post-incident investigations.",
    "Employees must be informed of workplace violence prevention plans and how to report violent incidents without fear of retaliation.",
    "SB 553 allows an employee representative to petition for a temporary workplace violence restraining order.",
    "Employers must annually review their workplace violence prevention plans.",
    "SB 553 will place the Workplace Violence General Industry Draft into effect on July 1, 2024.",
    "Cal/OSHA will continue its rulemaking process with input from workers, employers, and the public to refine a standard that keeps workers safe."
  ]
}
```
